header.filterPlaceholder: Substring; space means match all tokens…

metrics.hintDisconnected: Connect to pull /proc metrics from the machine where the agent runs.
metrics.cpuJiffiesNote: Cumulative jiffies from /proc/stat since boot — not live CPU %. Often HZ=100 so 1 jiffy ≈ 10 ms; in general 1 jiffy = 1/HZ second.

discover.hint: Click a row to fill a suggested command in the sidebar “CLI” box and copy it. Switching tabs clears the list—click List again.

cmd.title: Command (execute)
cmd.completionHint: Tab inserts the highlighted completion; ↑/↓ to move; click a row to insert. Enter runs the full line.

hookEditor.hintSec: BPF SEC("…") here is the ELF section; REPL --sec is a filter DSL — see docs/command-spec.md

sessionPanel.title: Session probes
sessionPanel.hintRepl: To add probes, open the sidebar “CLI” and run e.g. break <kprobe symbol>, trace pid tgid, watch <expr>. For tracepoints/uprobes, pick a row in “Find” to generate a hook add line. Full C programs: sidebar “Hook”.

tabHint.break: Kprobe breakpoints: stop the session on hits (debugger-style). Commands: break, delete, enable, disable.
tabHint.trace: Sample expressions in probe context (e.g. pid, regs). Command: trace.
tabHint.hook: Attached eBPF programs (template or full C), often via ringbuf. Commands: hook add, hook delete.
tabHint.watch: Emit when a watched expression’s value changes. Command: watch.

events.emptyDisconnected: Not connected. Connect to the agent, then click Start capture to see events here.
events.emptyNeedCapture: Connected, but capture is off. Click Start capture in the header to subscribe to the event stream.
events.emptyNoProbesYet:
Capture is on. If this stays empty, you likely have no probes attached yet:
• Sidebar “CLI”: break, trace, hook add, watch, …
• Sidebar “Hook”: compile & attach eBPF C
Events only appear when the kernel or target process actually hits those probes.

settings.title: Settings
settings.hintShortcut: Open this dialog anytime with ⌘ or Ctrl + comma (also under the View menu). Appearance and language are saved locally.

about.title: Phantom
about.subtitle: Remote eBPF debugging — desktop client
about.body: Phantom is a distributed debugger for Linux eBPF: a desktop client connects to a Phantom agent over the network to attach probes, run a GDB-style command interface, and stream structured events from the machine where the agent runs. This application is open source; contributions and feedback are welcome on GitHub.
about.licenseDetail: This software is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. You may obtain a copy of the License below. Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
about.repositoryHint: Browse source, report issues, and follow project updates on GitHub.
about.copyright: © {{year}} The Phantom Authors. All rights reserved.

glossary.loadavg_one: /proc/loadavg field 1: 1-minute load average (runnable + uninterruptible tasks, interpret per CPU count).
glossary.mem_total_kb: /proc/meminfo MemTotal: total physical memory reported by the kernel (kB).
glossary.mem_available_kb: /proc/meminfo MemAvailable: estimate of memory available for new workloads without swapping (includes reclaimable cache).
glossary.mem_buffers_kb: /proc/meminfo Buffers: raw block I/O cache (usually small).
glossary.mem_cached_kb: /proc/meminfo Cached: page cache and reclaimable slab; large values are often normal.
glossary.cpu_user: /proc/stat: time in user mode for non-niced processes (jiffies).
glossary.cpu_system: /proc/stat: system/kernel mode jiffies (excluding some interrupt accounting).
glossary.cpu_iowait: /proc/stat: time waiting for I/O (may be zero on some kernels/setups).
glossary.rx_bytes: /proc/net/dev: received bytes on the interface (since boot, cumulative).
glossary.vm_size_kb: /proc/<tid>/status VmSize: virtual address space size (VSZ), includes unmapped pages.
glossary.vm_rss_kb: /proc/<tid>/status VmRSS: resident set size mapped to physical memory (approx. RSS).
glossary.threads_count: /proc/<tid>/status Threads: number of tasks in the thread group (often identical on each TID line).
glossary.tid: Thread ID (kernel task id). TGID is the POSIX process ID; TIDs sharing a TGID are threads in one process.
glossary.tgid: Thread group ID: the traditional PID; matches tgid in BPF events.
